title: Tax Depot
short_description: The town's tax coins arrive — share them out so the fire station, school, roads and hospital each get exactly what they need before opening day.
description: Taxes are the coins a community pools together to pay for things everyone shares, and today you're in charge of the depot. A pile of tax coins comes in, each public service shows how many it needs — the fire station, the school, the roads, the hospital, the park — and you hand out coins with the plus and minus buttons until every service is funded exactly right and no coins are wasted. It teaches civics you can see: what governments actually spend taxes on, why public services matter, and the careful sharing-out that a budget takes. Grade bands ramp from three services with small needs, to four, to five bigger ones that make you plan. Funding the whole town efficiently and building streaks earns bonus points. A free online taxes, budgeting and public-services civics game for kids in grades 1–5, feeding your score and GP on OnlyGrins.
controls: Mouse / tap + and − on each service to give it tax coins until it is funded exactly.
